label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 9

extraction.one_line_summary: MENKES Louis won from the break on a 24% gradient finale while EVENEPOEL Remco distanced ROGLIC and MAS by attacking on the final climb.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step controlled peloton tempo for Evenepoel despite teammate Serry's COVID withdrawal, showing organizational depth.
Evenepoel attacked final 24% climb when DSM's Arensman wasn't in break, exploiting their missed tactical opportunity.
Breakaway split before final climb from internal dissent over work rate between Ballestero and Janssens.
Pre-climb peloton crash opened gaps that favored breakaway survival, nerves fraying before selection point.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Looked comfortable distancing GC rivals on 24% gradients while attacking, suggesting superior power-to-weight on extreme pitches
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: Lost 1:12 to Evenepoel on final climb, biggest GC loser suggesting form deterioration or injury
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: Found life hard on final climb, lost 44 seconds to Evenepoel despite staying with him longer than Roglic
rider_signals.ARENSMAN Thymen: negative: DSM kept him out of break despite being 3:18 down, tactical error as break succeeded
rider_signals.MENKES Louis: positive: First grand tour stage win at 30, dominated breakaway on brutal finale showing late-career form peak
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Lost 2:37 to winner despite winning this stage in 2018, struggling on extreme gradients
